summary: S-priority unverified claims are mostly verifiable as bounded, stratified, empirical programs. Absolute worldwide completeness and production-grade security cannot be claimed from the theory repository alone.

items.minimumDataset: A stratified gold set by country, region type, address type, natural feature type, and indoor or vertical unit type.
items.passCriterion: For each declared stratum, the target referent appears in the candidate set at the stated recall threshold, or the resolver returns unresolved without identifier issuance.
items.safeWording: Candidate-generation sufficiency is a regional and source-bound assumption. Coverage is evaluated by strata; insufficient coverage must produce unresolved or additional-evidence-required.
items.unsafeWording: AMT can find every addressable object worldwide.
items.firstExperiment: Build a 10-country fixture covering urban units, rural villages, islands, no-postcode areas, natural features, and renamed places.

items.claim: Multilingual expansion improves address search recall
items.minimumDataset: Paired aliases and queries across native script, Latin transliteration, English, historical names, common abbreviations, and dialect variants.
items.passCriterion: Recall improves over the baseline for declared strata while false merges remain under the declared threshold.
items.safeWording: Multilingual expansion is a recall layer. It does not preserve identity by itself and must feed a second-stage structural and source-bound resolver.
items.unsafeWording: Translation proves two address strings are the same address.
items.firstExperiment: Create fixtures for Japanese, English, Chinese, Korean, Arabic, Cyrillic, Latin transliteration, and historical place-name variants.

items.claim: Worldwide natural and cultural feature addressability
items.minimumDataset: Feature fixtures for rivers, waterfalls, lakes, islands, deserts, wetlands, glaciers, caves, valleys, heritage sites, ports, and mountain areas.
items.passCriterion: Each declared feature type has source identifiers, geometry or representative point policy, multilingual names, and an explicit ambiguity policy.
items.safeWording: AMT can model natural and cultural referents when source-bound feature data and geometry policies exist. Worldwide exhaustive recognition remains an empirical target.
items.unsafeWording: AMT recognizes every natural and cultural name worldwide.
items.firstExperiment: Start with islands, ports, lakes, mountains, deserts, and UNESCO-style cultural sites because their geometry and naming policies expose different failure modes.

items.claim: Strict GIS validation with no hard errors and controlled warnings
items.minimumDataset: Country or region packs with declared geometry source, license, coordinate reference system, topology policy, and warning budget.
items.passCriterion: Hard errors equal zero; warning count is below the declared budget; every warning is classified by type and owner.
items.safeWording: Machine-readability and hard-error-free validation can be claimed for named packs. Strict global validation is unfinished until warning budgets are closed globally.
items.unsafeWording: GIS validation proves worldwide geography is correct.
items.firstExperiment: Choose one small island pack, one dense city pack, one mountain or desert pack, and one disputed-region policy pack.

items.claim: Comparison against commercial address validation APIs
items.minimumDataset: A rights-cleared benchmark with identical inputs, permitted API use, no private recipient data, and country-purpose stratification.
items.passCriterion: AMT reports condition-by-condition comparison without publishing proprietary responses or claiming universal superiority.
items.safeWording: AMT should be compared by evaluation condition: transparency, abstention, lineage, natural-feature handling, privacy boundaries, and address validation accuracy.
items.unsafeWording: AMT beats all commercial address APIs.
items.firstExperiment: Implement dry-run adapters for Loqate, Experian, Melissa, and Smarty that record only normalized metrics after terms and credential approval.

items.minimumDataset: Toy circuit fixtures, public inputs, commitments, nullifier scopes, revocation roots, and negative tests without witness material.
items.passCriterion: Only ZK-ready can be claimed until a circuit, proof system, negative tests, and external cryptographic review exist.
items.safeWording: AMT defines address predicates and proof-ready envelopes. A complete ZK system requires audited circuits and proof-system security review.
items.unsafeWording: AMT already provides complete zero-knowledge address proofs.
items.firstExperiment: Define one residence-country predicate and one delivery-zone predicate with toy commitments and explicit non-public witness handling.

items.minimumDataset: Threat model cases for keys, revocation, QR replay, sync, delegation, public/private boundaries, connector errors, and audit logs.
items.passCriterion: Production-security claims remain blocked until key management, revocation, replay prevention, connector boundaries, and external review pass.
items.safeWording: AMT can define the security semantics that AGID/AOID should implement. Production security belongs to the protocol implementation and external audit process.
items.unsafeWording: AGID/AOID are production secure because AMT is defined.
items.firstExperiment: Create a STRIDE-style threat model and local no-raw, no-replay, no-unsafe-retry, redaction, and revocation test matrix.
